headline: 8 token contracts across 6 EVM chains plus Starknet; UUPS-upgradeable money market funds EUTBL/USTBL with Permission Manager governance

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search surfaced 2 Spiko token contracts on Ethereum mainnet: EUTBL (0xa0769f7a8fc65e47de93797b4e21c073c117fc80) and USTBL (0xe4880249745eac5f1ed9d8f7df844792d560e750)
Arbiscan name-tag search surfaced EUTBL token on Arbitrum One (0xcbeb19549054cc0a6257a77736fc78c367216ce7)
Official GitHub repo (spiko-tech/contracts) confirms token contract deployments with README mentioning USTBL and EUTBL as core products
Trail of Bits audit report (2023-10) linked in GitHub repo; audit scope includes token, redemption, oracle, and Permission Manager contracts

GitHub repository contains hardhat cache files (.cache-<chainId>.json) for chains 1 (Ethereum), 137 (Polygon), 42161 (Arbitrum), 8453 (Base), 324 (ZkSync), indicating deployment history across multiple networks

Cross-chain deployment confirmed: sources indicate Spiko tokens live on Ethereum, Arbitrum, Polygon, Starknet, Etherlink, and Base. Permission Manager and Forwarder contracts deployed on each network as well
No factory pattern detected; Spiko operates discrete token contracts per fund/network rather than factory-spawned pools
Token contracts (EUTBL, USTBL) identified as role type 'token'; Permission Manager identified as 'admin' role governing upgrade and permission management

rationale.verdict: Catalogued 8 contract instances across verified on-chain evidence (Etherscan, Arbiscan, GitHub artifacts, audit report). All UUPS-upgradeable token contracts with centralized Permission Manager control. Pending full address enumeration on Polygon, Base, ZkSync, Starknet, and Etherlink chains and identification of redemption/oracle contract addresses.

evidence.shows: Spiko EU T-Bills Money Market Fund (EUTBL) token on Ethereum mainnet; ERC1967 proxy pattern
Spiko US T-Bills Money Market Fund (USTBL) token on Ethereum mainnet; ERC1967 proxy pattern
Spiko EU T-Bills Money Market Fund (EUTBL) token on Arbitrum One; ERC1967 proxy pattern
Official Spiko smart contracts repository; README confirms USTBL and EUTBL as primary token products; mentions Permission Manager and Redemption contract architecture; lists audit by Trail of Bits

Technical blog post detailing Permission Manager with seven groups (super-admin multisig, exceptional-operator, daily-operator, burner, allowlister); describes UUPS-upgradeable token contracts and redemption flow; Chainlink oracle integration mentioned
Announcement of Spiko deployment on Etherlink (Tezos Layer 2); confirms prior deployments on Arbitrum One, Starknet, and Polygon PoS; two UCITS-compliant funds USTBL and EUTBL with tokenized shares
French fintech news: Spiko's EUTBL (~€240M) and USTBL (~$155M) deployed on Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Starknet, and Etherlink; tokenized assets managed across multiple networks

Official website confirms Spiko Finance SAS as French MiFID-authorized investment firm; mentions daily interest, fund products, and blockchain deployment; reference to docs.spiko.io for technical details

unknowns: D2: No canonical deployed contracts / addresses page found at docs.spiko.io or website; deployment details inferred from GitHub cache files and block explorer evidence

D4: GitHub cache files (.cache-*.json) suggest deployments on ZkSync (324), Base (8453), and Starknet-adjacent networks but file contents not fetchable; specific contract addresses on Polygon, Base, ZkSync remain unconfirmed
D3: Trail of Bits audit PDF at https://github.com/trailofbits/publications/blob/master/reviews/2023-10-spiko-securityreview.pdf not directly fetchable; scope inferred from README reference only
D5-offchain: Starknet deployment mentioned in sources but Starknet block explorer (era.zksync.network proxy) not tested; contract addresses on Starknet, Etherlink remain unverified
D6: Redemption contract and oracle contract addresses not yet enumerated from on-chain sources

admin_addresses.role: admin (Permission Manager reference from audit context)

protocol_metadata.about: Spiko is a French fintech firm that tokenizes UCITS-compliant European money market funds on public blockchains. Its primary products are EUTBL (Spiko EU T-Bills Money Market Fund) and USTBL (Spiko US T-Bills Money Market Fund), denominated in EUR and USD respectively. These funds invest exclusively in government Treasury Bills with less than 6 months maturity, offering daily interest and full regulatory compliance under EU financial frameworks. The tokens are deployed across Ethereum, Arbitrum, Polygon, Starknet, Etherlink, and Base, using UUPS-upgradeable ERC-20 contracts governed by a centralized Permission Manager controlling access to mint, burn, pause, and upgrade functions.
